channel.title: EURUSD News Feed
channel.description: Latest EURUSD headlines from investingLive, ForexLive, ForexCrunch, ActionForex, Investing.com, DailyForex and Trading Economics

item.title: [TradingEconomics] Euro Bounces Back as Middle East Hopes Rise
[investingLive] What are the bias, risks, and key targets for the EURUSD, USDJPY, and GBPUSD right now?
item.description: As the new trading day gets underway, markets are off to a relatively cautious and choppy start. Oil prices are modestly lower and trading near session lows after giving back an earlier spike higher. U.S. yields are also slightly lower — down around 1 basis point across much of the curve after tradi

item.title: [investingLive] EURUSD bangs against target resistance at 1.1655. Can the buyers get through the target.
item.description: In the morning kickstart video, I commented on the 1.1655 level as a key target. The infograph highlights my key technical comment on that pair (see highlighted yellow are on the infograph below from the start of the trading day):The price did move higher and tested that level in the North American

item.title: [TradingEconomics] Euro Near One-Month Low Amid Middle East Risks
[investingLive] investingLive European FX news wrap: UK jobs data disappoints, US dollar remains supported
item.description: Nasdaq's bullish momentum stalls as downside risks mount: pause or start of a correction?Euro area trade surplus narrows in March as energy deficit widens on Middle East conflictOil prices remain persistently elevated amid prolonged US-Iran stalemateThere is potential for a strong dollar rally this

item.title: [ActionForex] EUR/USD Daily Outlook
item.description: Intraday bias in EUR/USD remains neutral as consolidations continue above 1.1607 temporary low. Risk will stay on the downside as long as 55 4H EMA (now at 1.1686) holds. Rebound from 1.1408 could have completed as a corrective three-wave move. Break of 1.1607 will bring deeper fall to retest 1.1408

item.title: [FXStreet] Canadian Dollar gains against Euro despite softer domestic inflation data
item.description: The Euro (EUR) weakens against the Canadian Dollar (CAD) on Tuesday as rising Oil prices linked to the US-Iran conflict continue to support the commodity-linked Loonie, even as the latest Canadian inflation data came in softer than expected.

item.title: [investingLive] EURUSD seller take the pair to the lowest level since April 8th. Sellers in control.
item.description: The EURUSD is extending lower, falling to a new session low at 1.1593 after failing to sustain an earlier upside break in the early Asian-Pacific session. In the early trading today, the pair traded as high as 1.1661 briefly pushing above a key swing area ceiling near 1.1655, a level that had capped
